cit.basis: Flat 10% corporate income tax levied at entity level; both entities apply the same rate.
cit.note: Two-entity fiscal system: FBiH and Republika Srpska both levy 10% profit tax under their own laws (the Brčko District broadly follows entity rules). Because the rate is identical, the estimator applies a single flat 10%.

vat.note: 17% VAT is unified and administered state-wide by the Indirect Taxation Authority (UINO/ITA).

personal_income_tax.note: Personal income tax is levied at entity level (broadly a flat 10%); not modelled here.

official_sources.name: Indirect Taxation Authority of Bosnia and Herzegovina (UINO/ITA)

metadata.caveat: AI-estimated: rates and rules in this pack were generated by an AI system and have NOT been verified against primary legislation. The two-entity structure (FBiH / Republika Srpska) means direct-tax rules are set by separate entity laws; VAT is state-level via UINO/ITA. Treat every figure as an unverified estimate and confirm against the Indirect Taxation Authority (https://www.uino.gov.ba/), the relevant entity tax administration, and a local advisor before any filing or production use.

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year; jurisdiction fiscal calendars and transitional periods may differ.

rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
Entity-specific direct-tax rules (FBiH vs Republika Srpska vs Brčko District), incentives, WHT, and filing obligations may alter outcomes.
Verify against official guidance and a local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
